title: What makes an idea feel aligned
subtitle: A journal set for mapping how you recognise a good money idea when it arrives.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 43 of the MONEY program. The prompts explore how profitable ideas register in you before logic catches up with them.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 43 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to understand how you currently filter ideas — what gets through and what doesn't.

content.text: A journal set from Day 43 of the MONEY program. The focus is on how you recognise a profitable idea before you've had time to reason it through — and what tends to interrupt that recognition.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What does an exciting idea feel like, before you've had time to assess it?
Write whatever comes. Physical, practical, anything. Don't filter for whether the idea was good or not.
Think of one idea you've had recently that you let pass without pursuing. What happened?
Write what came between the idea and the decision not to follow it.
What makes an idea feel exciting and aligned for you?
Write the markers you actually use, not the ones you think you should use.
How do you typically distinguish doubt from a genuine no?
Write what the difference feels like when you can see it clearly.
What ideas, insights, or inspirations have come to you recently that you feel grateful for?
Write them down. Small counts as much as large here.
Adapted from Day 43 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
